data.title: Dycom Industries reports Q4 34.4% revenue growth, $1.46B revenues, $162.4M EBITDA, $2.03 EPS, $9.5B backlog, and $709.2M cash.
data.description: Dycom Industries, Inc. shared its fourth-quarter results along with forecasts for fiscal 2027. Contract revenues surged by 34.4% year over year to $1.46 billion, surpassing analyst projections of $1.35 billion. Organic revenue saw a 16.6% Y/Y increase. Adjusted EBITDA jumped by 39.6% Y/Y to $162.4 million, amounting to 11.1% of contract revenues. The adjusted EPS stood at $2.03, beating estimates of $1.73. Operating cash flow for the quarter totaled $419.0 million. The company closed the quarter with a backlog of $9.5 billion and cash reserves of $709.2 million. In the same quarter of fiscal 2026, Dycom Industries acquired Power Solutions, a company specializing in electrical infrastructure for data centers and critical facilities in the Washington D.C. area.

data.title: Rigetti Computing (NASDAQ: RGTI) stock rises ahead of Q4 earnings release. Expecting loss of 4 cents/share on $2.46 million revenue. Investors watch for guidance.
data.description: Rigetti Computing Inc's stock (NASDAQ: RGTI) is increasing today as investors are preparing for the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report after the market closes. Analysts anticipate a quarterly loss of 4 cents per share with revenue around $2.46 million. The market is anticipating potential positive surprises or guidance, given the stock's previous slide despite a modest EPS beat last quarter. This indicates investors' optimism towards the company's long-term growth prospects.

data.title: French company files an amendment to its annual financial report in its "Document d’Enregistrement Universel".
data.description: An amendment has been filed to the French "Document d’Enregistrement Universel," which includes the Annual Financial Report. This update provides crucial financial information and insights regarding the company's performance. The amendment filing underscores the importance of transparency and compliance with regulatory requirements. Investors and stakeholders should take note of this development as it impacts their understanding of the company's operations and financial health. It is a significant step in maintaining regulatory standards and ensuring accurate reporting. This update enhances the transparency and credibility of the company's financial information, thereby fostering trust among investors. Stay informed and keep track of further developments regarding this amendment to the Annual Financial Report.

data.title: Clean Simple Eats launches new peach-flavored clear protein powder, expanding its high-quality protein line.
data.description: Clean Simple Eats (CSE), a prominent brand in the health and wellness sector, has introduced a new peach-flavored protein powder to its range of delicious and nutritious protein products. This addition further expands CSE's selection of high-quality, light, and juicy protein offerings. The company, based in Salt Lake City, made this announcement on March 4, 2026. Known for its top-notch products, CSE remains dedicated to providing the best-tasting clean protein options to its customers.

data.title: Bathroom remodeling expert Jeff McDonald from Saratoga, NY, emphasizes the importance of layout planning in HelloNation article.
data.description: In Saratoga, New York on March 4, 2026, research conducted by HelloNation revealed that the key factor in a bathroom remodel is effective layout planning. This information was shared in a recent HelloNation article which emphasizes the importance of carefully mapping out the design before selecting finishes. Proper layout planning was underscored as crucial for achieving a successful renovation project.

data.title: Broadcast Journalist Joan Lunden to discuss her memoir "Joan: Life Beyond the Script" at National Press Club on Mar. 19, 2026.
data.description: Renowned journalist Joan Lunden, known for her time as host of Good Morning America, is set to share insights from her memoir "Joan: Life Beyond the Script" at the upcoming Headliners Book Event on March 19, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. Interested individuals can secure tickets to this event. Lunden, who has vast experience in reporting, will delve into her life and career during the event.

data.title: AI DraftNEPABench project, a partnership between OpenAI and PNNL, aims to reduce federal permitting time by 15%, saving 1-5 hours per review.
data.description: OpenAI and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory have collaborated on the launch of DraftNEPABench, a tool demonstrating that AI agents have the potential to reduce the time spent on federal environmental reviews by 1 to 5 hours per subsection. This innovative technology aims to streamline and expedite the review process, offering significant time-saving benefits for stakeholders involved in environmental assessments.

data.title: Review finds ongoing fatal crash trends in California due to distracted and impaired driving. Public safety concern with fatalities continuing.
data.description: On March 4, 2026, in Los Angeles, reports show that fatal motor vehicle accidents are still a major issue in California, with distracted and impaired driving being the main causes. Publicly available traffic safety data highlights these factors as significant contributors to the ongoing problem.

data.title: Michael Saylor of Strategy (NASDAQ: MSTR) reiterates buy the dip strategy as BTC surpasses $73,000, plans major Bitcoin purchase.
data.description: Michael Saylor, the chairman of Strategy (NASDAQ: MSTR), reiterated his bullish stance by advocating for a "buy the dip / stay long" approach as the company prepares for another substantial Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) acquisition. Saylor recently took to X on Mar. 3 to question if others are following his lead in purchasing Bitcoin at the present moment, indicating his belief in the value of current levels despite the short-term market fluctuations. This decision is consistent with his established strategy of steadily accumulating BTC both on a personal level and through company investments. This development underscores Saylor's unwavering confidence in Bitcoin's potential, pushing for continued investment in the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Investing during a conflict in the Persian Gulf: Key strategy is to stay focused on financial goals amid tensions.
data.description: Recent U.S. conflicts have seen a trend of downplaying or ignoring their occurrence. This approach to handling conflicts has been observed to be most effective. However, with the current situation, there is a question arising: will this strategy be altered?

data.title: Crypto philanthropy gains traction with over $100M in stablecoin donations to charities via Giving Block in 2025, spurred by US legal shifts.
data.description: In 2025, the fundraising platform Giving Block revealed that it had helped facilitate over $100 million in donations of stablecoins to various charities. This significant increase in donations may have been influenced by recent changes in US laws.
